steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The left side forms an independent clause ending with the predicate adjective “threefold.” The right side is a participial phrase starting with “expanding” that elaborates on what “threefold” means.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A colon introduces elaboration or explanation, explaining what “threefold” means by introducing the three dimensions that follow.

A semicolon incorrectly suggests two independent clauses; a period creates a sentence fragment; and no punctuation creates an awkward construction.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
